hints: Try using a hash map to map values to their indices.
title: Two Sum
description: Given an array of integers nums and an integer target, return indices of the two numbers such that they add up to target. You may assume that each input would have exactly one solution, and you may not use the same element twice.
optimalApproach: Store complement (target - nums[i]) in a HashMap. Time complexity is O(N).

hints: Use a Stack to push opening brackets and pop to match.
title: Valid Parentheses
description: Given a string s containing just the characters '(', ')', '{', '}', '[' and ']', determine if the input string is valid. An input string is valid if opening brackets are closed by the same type of brackets and closed in the correct order.
constraintsText:
1 <= s.length <= 10^4
s consists of parentheses only.
examples: [{"input":"s = \"()[]{}\"","output":"true","explanation":"Each bracket is matched with its corresponding closing bracket."}]
optimalApproach: Use a stack. Push opening characters. Pop and check equivalence when closing characters are found.

hints: Use a dummy head node to simplify head boundary tracking.
title: Merge Two Sorted Lists
description: You are given the heads of two sorted linked lists list1 and list2. Merge the two lists into one sorted list. The list should be made by splicing together the nodes of the first two lists.
constraintsText:
The number of nodes in both lists is in the range [0, 50].
-100 <= Node.val <= 100
examples: [{"input":"list1 = [1,2,4], list2 = [1,3,4]","output":"[1,1,2,3,4,4]","explanation":"Spliced list nodes together in sorted order."}]
optimalApproach: Compare values at heads of list1 and list2. Spliced into target dummy node. Advance pointer.

hints: Try to calculate the prefix sum. If it goes negative, reset it to zero.
title: Maximum Subarray
description: Given an integer array nums, find the subarray with the largest sum, and return its sum. (Also known as Kadanes Algorithm).
optimalApproach: Kadanes Algorithm. Maintain current sum and max sum. Reset current sum to 0 if it drops below 0.

hints: Use a combination of a Doubly Linked List and a HashMap.
title: LRU Cache
description: Design a data structure that follows the constraints of a Least Recently Used (LRU) cache. Implement get(key) and put(key, value) operations in O(1) time complexity.
optimalApproach: Map provides O(1) lookup. Doubly LinkedList tracks the access history, adding to head and evicting from tail.

hints: Use a Queue to hold nodes at the current level.
title: Binary Tree Level Order Traversal
description: Given the root of a binary tree, return the level order traversal of its nodes' values. (i.e., from left to right, level by level).
constraintsText:
The number of nodes in the tree is in the range [0, 2000].
-1000 <= Node.val <= 1000
optimalApproach: Use a FIFO Queue. For each level, record its size, dequeue all elements of the current level, and enqueue their children.

hints: This is a classic 2D Dynamic Programming problem.
title: Edit Distance
description: Given two strings word1 and word2, return the minimum number of operations required to convert word1 to word2. You have the following three operations permitted on a word: Insert a character, Delete a character, Replace a character.
constraintsText:
0 <= word1.length, word2.length <= 500
Strings consist of lowercase English letters.
optimalApproach: Let dp[i][j] represent edit distance of prefix s1[0...i] and s2[0...j]. Fill table iteratively: if equal, dp[i][j]=dp[i-1][j-1], else 1 + min of insert, delete, replace.
